🧭 GPS Mission Control

Your step by step walkthrough for the current phase. Check off steps as you complete them, track boss readiness, and navigate with real game maps.

HOW TO USE GPS

Steps appear 3 at a time. Click the checkbox on each step to mark it complete. The map shows pins for each step location. Use the phase selector to jump between phases or browse ahead.

TIP: Use the "/" key to open Quick Update for fast level changes, catches, and item pickups without switching tabs.

Active Mission

NEXT 3 STEPS
1 / 6
Phase Progress 0 / 0

🧠 Battle Advisor

▼

🗺 Browse All Maps

▼
🚨
Don't Press B!
When Mankey reaches Lv 28, let it evolve into Primeape. When Nidoran male reaches Lv 16, let it evolve into Nidorino. These are NOT the Pokemon you should cancel evolution on. Save the Moon Stone for after Nidorino learns the moves you want.

⚔️ Squad + God Tier Blueprint

Manage your active team of 6 and track progress toward building the ultimate God Tier squad. Edit levels and moves, then Save to update readiness gauges everywhere.

SQUAD MANAGEMENT

Edit your team's names, levels, and moves below. Hit "Save Squad" to push changes to the boss readiness gauge, damage calcs, and evolution engine. The Blueprint cards below show your target end-game team. Click a card to mark it as obtained.

TIP: Type Coverage Heatmap at the bottom shows gaps in your team's offensive coverage across all 17 types.
🎮 My Current Squad
✓ Saved & Readiness Updated

📋 God-Tier Blueprint

💡
Switch Rewind = Perfect Natures
Save state before every key encounter. Re-roll until you land the target Nature. Click a card to mark it as obtained.
Nature Watch
🔴
Nidoran male | Double Kick at Level 12
Verified against PokemonDB Gen 3 FRLG learnset: Nidoran male learns Double Kick at Level 12 (not Lv 22). This is Fighting type (Physical in Gen 3), super effective vs Rock. This is your Brock backup plan.
🔴
Points of No Return | Upcoming
S.S. Anne: Explore EVERY room before talking to the Captain. Ship leaves permanently after you get HM01 Cut.
Snorlax: Only 2 in the entire game. Save before each. If you KO them, they're gone forever.
Eevee: One from Celadon Mansion. Save before pickup to rewind for Modest/Timid Nature (for Jolteon).
🗺️ Team Type Coverage

Green = you have a super effective move. Red = no coverage anywhere on your team. Based on your current roster's moves.

🏆 Gym Tracker

Track your progress through all 8 Kanto gym leaders plus the Elite Four. Click a gym card to mark it cleared. Strategy details are on the GPS tab's readiness gauge.

GYM BATTLES

Each card shows the gym leader, their type specialty, and their ace Pokemon's level. Click to toggle cleared status. The GPS tab's "Am I Ready?" gauge gives you detailed matchup analysis and team recommendations for whichever gym you're approaching next.

TIP: Use the Simulate button on the GPS readiness gauge to see turn by turn damage projections against any gym leader.

📋 Full Route Checklist

Complete list of every task in the selected phase. Use the search bar to filter by keyword or the tag buttons to filter by type. The GPS tab shows these 3 at a time with maps.

CHECKLIST GUIDE

Every step in your God Run is listed here per phase. Filter by Catch, Battle, Hidden items, Critical steps, Items, or NPC interactions. The area completion audit at the top shows your progress per location.

TIP: Critical (red) steps are things you cannot miss or go back for. Prioritize these.

📖 Pokedex Tracker

Track every Pokemon you've caught, their levels, and party status. Essential for the 100% completionist goal of catching all 151 Kanto Pokemon.

POKEDEX TRACKING

Add Pokemon as you catch them using the form below. Each entry tracks dex number, name, catch location, and level. The party badge indicates Pokemon currently in your active squad.

TIP: Quick Update (/) supports "caught Pikachu" or "+Pikachu" to add catches without opening this tab.
Add Pokemon
All Pokemon

🧬 Evolution Lab

Decision engine for when to evolve and what moves to watch for. Checks your squad's levels against evolution thresholds and critical move learn points.

EVOLUTION DECISIONS

The engine checks each squad member and tells you whether it's SAFE to evolve now, if you should WAIT for a critical move, or if evolution is N/A. The Move Timeline below shows every upcoming level up move with critical ones highlighted in red.

TIP: Evolution decisions update automatically when you save your roster on the Squad tab.

📜 Move Learning Timeline

Upcoming moves for each squad member. Critical moves highlighted in red. Learned moves shown as crossed out. Evolution points marked in purple.

🎒 Bag + Money Manager

Track your inventory, manage your funds, and plan upcoming shop purchases. Tag items as Keep, Sell, Toss, or Key Item for organization.

INVENTORY MANAGEMENT

Add items with quantities and tags. The Money Tracker below tracks your balance with a full transaction log. The Shop Planner shows upcoming purchases by phase with red highlights for items you can't afford yet.

TIP: Quick Update supports "+Potion 3" to add items and "$+500" or "money +500" to update your balance.
Add Item
💰 Money Tracker
¥
🛒 Shop Planner

Key purchases by phase. Red items exceed your current balance.

🧪 Move Decision Lab

Compare a new move against your current moveset. Accounts for Gen 3 physical/special split by TYPE, STAB bonuses, and stat synergy. Includes a quick damage calculator.

MOVE ANALYSIS

Select a squad member, pick a new move to evaluate, and choose which slot to replace. The lab will tell you whether to LEARN, SKIP, or consider it SITUATIONAL based on power, type coverage, and stat synergy. The damage calc below lets you estimate specific damage against any target.

TIP: In Gen 3, physical vs special is determined by TYPE, not the individual move. All Fire moves are Special, all Fighting moves are Physical.
🔬 Analyze a Move
💥 Quick Damage Calc

Estimate damage as % of target HP. Uses Gen 3 formula with approximated base stats.

💿 TM/HM Tracker

Track which TMs and HMs you have found, used, or taught. TMs are single use in Gen 3. HMs cannot be deleted until the Move Deleter in Fuchsia City.

TM/HM MANAGEMENT

Click the status badge on each TM to cycle through: Not Found, Found, and Used. You can also record which Pokemon you taught it to. Filter by TMs only, HMs only, or by found/not found status. Each entry shows the move name, type, power, and where to find it.

TIP: TMs are one time use in Gen 3. Plan carefully before using key TMs like Earthquake, Thunderbolt, and Ice Beam.

📊 EV Training

Track Effort Values per squad member. Max 252 per stat, 510 total. Optional for casual play but essential for min-maxing your God Tier team.

EV TRAINING (ADVANCED)

Effort Values are hidden stats that grow as you defeat Pokemon. Each Pokemon you KO gives EVs in specific stats. Every 4 EVs = +1 stat point at Lv 100. Max 252 per stat, 510 total across all stats. Edit values directly per squad member.

TIP: For a casual God Run, you don't need to min-max EVs. But if you want to optimize, focus on each Pokemon's primary attack stat and Speed. Route training suggestions appear below each card.

⚙️ Gen 3 Quick Reference

Essential mechanics, natures cheat sheet, points of no return, session notes, run statistics, and save management. Everything you need to know about Gen 3 FireRed in one place.

GEN 3 MECHANICS

The most critical thing in Gen 3: Physical vs Special is determined by TYPE, not by individual move. This means Earthquake (Ground) is always Physical and Flamethrower (Fire) is always Special. This changes everything about how you build movesets.

TIP: Use Session Notes below to jot down where you stopped, what you need to do next, or any observations during play.
Physical / Special Split | By TYPE
⚠️
The #1 Rule
In Gen 3, Physical vs Special is determined by the move's TYPE, not the individual move. This changes everything about how you build movesets.
Physical
Normal Fighting Flying Ground Rock Bug Ghost Poison Steel
Attack stat -> Defense stat
Special
Fire Water Grass Electric Psychic Ice Dragon Dark
Sp. Atk stat -> Sp. Def stat
Key Implications for Your Team
Nidoking
Earthquake = Ground = Physical (great, uses his Attack). Thunderbolt = Electric = Special (mediocre, wastes his stats). Build around Physical moves.
Charizard
Flamethrower = Fire = Special (perfect, uses Sp.Atk). Aerial Ace = Flying = Physical (mismatch). Focus on Fire/Dragon special moves.
Alakazam
All Psychic moves = Special. His Sp.Atk is massive. Perfect synergy. Psychic + Shadow Ball (Ghost = Physical, but no better option).
Jolteon
Thunderbolt = Electric = Special. Thunder = Electric = Special. Perfect for Jolteon's huge Sp.Atk/Speed.
Natures Cheat Sheet
Adamant
+Attack / -Sp.Atk. Best for: Nidoking, Snorlax.
Modest
+Sp.Atk / -Attack. Best for: Alakazam, Jolteon, Lapras.
Timid
+Speed / -Attack. Best for: Charizard, Alakazam, Jolteon (if you want to outspeed everything).
Jolly
+Speed / -Sp.Atk. Best for: Physical attackers who need speed. Less relevant for your team.
Careful
+Sp.Def / -Sp.Atk. Best for: Snorlax (tank variant).
Points of No Return
S.S. Anne
Ship departs forever after getting HM01 Cut from the Captain. Explore every cabin, fight every trainer, grab every item FIRST.
Snorlax x2
Route 12 and Route 16. Only two in the game. KO = gone forever. SAVE before each encounter.
Lapras
Gift from NPC on Silph Co. 7F. One time. Save before talking to rewind for Nature.
Eevee
One from Celadon Mansion rooftop. You need this for Jolteon. Save before pickup.
Fossils
Mt. Moon: Helix Fossil (Omanyte) OR Dome Fossil (Kabuto). Pick Helix for completionist value (Omastar is rarer in trades).
Legendaries
Zapdos, Articuno, Moltres, Mewtwo. One encounter each. Save before every single one.
📝 Session Notes

Free form notes. Auto saved as you type.

Export your entire dashboard state as a JSON file for backup. Import to restore from a previous backup.

🗄️ Data Admin

Search, view, and edit all 151 Pokemon stats, 322 moves, learnsets, evolution data, and encounter tables. For power users who want to correct or customize the underlying game data.

DATA MANAGEMENT

Search for any Pokemon or move to view its full stats, types, learnset, and evolution chain. You can manually edit any value. Overrides are highlighted in yellow and persist in localStorage. Use "Reset to Default" to restore PokeAPI source data.

TIP: All data was sourced from PokeAPI and corrected for Gen 3 mechanics (Fairy type removed, move powers adjusted, physical/special split by type). Edits here propagate to all calculators and engines.
Search Pokemon or Moves
Data Source
All 151 Kanto Pokemon stats, 322 moves, FireRed learnsets, evolution chains, EV yields, and encounter data sourced from PokeAPI.
Gen 3 physical/special split applied by TYPE (not modern damage class).
Sprites: FireRed/LeafGreen generation from PokeAPI sprite repository.
Manual overrides are highlighted in yellow and persist in localStorage. Use "Reset to Default" to restore PokeAPI values.

⚡ Quick Update

Formats: Name Level · caught Name / +Name · item Name x# / +Name # · money +# / $+# · beat Leader / gym Leader
Separate multiple commands with semicolons.
